basic_details.job_title: RITES Limited Engineer Recruitment 2026: Resident, Quality & Field Engineer (Civil) Vacancy in Kerala
basic_details.post_name: Resident Engineer (Civil), Quality Engineer (Civil), Field Engineer (Civil)
basic_details.short_information: RITES Limited, a Navratna Central Public Sector Enterprise under the Ministry of Railways, has released a recruitment notification for Engineering Professionals to be engaged on a contract basis through interview. The hiring is for project sites in Kerala and covers three Civil Engineering positions: Resident Engineer, Quality Engineer, and Field Engineer. Online applications open on 10 July 2026 and close on 30 July 2026. Candidates can find complete details on eligibility, vacancy break-up, age limit, remuneration, and the application process below.

important_dates.1: Commencement of submission of online application
important_dates.2: Last date of submission of online application (VC No. CL/04/26 to CL/06/26)

application_fee.details: There is no application fee for any category of candidates applying for VC No. CL/04/26 to CL/06/26.

age_limit.details: Resident Engineer (Civil) – CL/04/26: 55 years Quality Engineer (Civil) – CL/05/26: 55 years Field Engineer (Civil) – CL/06/26: 55 years | Age is reckoned as on the cut-off date of 30 July 2026. | PwBD candidates with not less than 40% of the relevant disability are eligible for the 10-year age relaxation, applicable irrespective of whether the post is reserved, provided the post is identified as suitable for PwBD.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Full-time Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering
Full-time Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering
Full-time Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering

qualification.details: Nationality requirements were not specified in the official notification provided. Candidates are advised to verify this detail on the official RITES website before applying. | The degree must be recognised by AICTE and awarded by a University established under a Central or State Act, an institution established by an Act of Parliament, or a Deemed University under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956. Sections A & B examinations of the Institution of Engineers (India), with enrolment on or before 31.05.2013, are treated as equivalent to a degree and accepted. Periods of training (other than on-the-job training), internship, teaching, academic work, fellowship, or PhD research are not counted as work experience.

salary_details.details: Field Engineer (Civil), minimum 2 years' experience: Basic Pay ₹23,340/month; Gross Pay ₹42,478/month; Yearly CTC ₹5,09,741 Quality Engineer (Civil), minimum 5 years' experience: Basic Pay ₹25,504/month; Gross Pay ₹46,417/month; Yearly CTC ₹5,57,008 Resident Engineer (Civil), minimum 8 years' experience: Basic Pay ₹27,869/month; Gross Pay ₹50,721/month; Yearly CTC ₹6,08,658 | The remuneration figures are indicative. Actual pay will depend on the place of posting and other terms and conditions of appointment.

selection_process: Communication & Competency: 35%. Minimum qualifying marks in the interview: 60% for UR/EWS candidates and 50% for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PWD candidates against reserved posts. There is no minimum qualifying mark required in the aggregate. Eligible candidates who successfully submit the online application are provisionally allowed for Document Scrutiny/Verification; only shortlisted/eligible candidates then appear for the Interview. RITES prepares a category-wise merit list of empanelled candidates and shares CVs with the client in order of merit. The final offer of engagement depends on approval of the CV by the client. Selected candidates must be found medically fit as per RITES Rules and Standards of Medical Fitness for the relevant post. Candidates may appear for the interview in either Hindi or English. Engagement is purely on contract basis
extendable until completion of the assignment subject to mutual consent and satisfactory performance. Selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India as per company requirement
though this recruitment is specifically for deployment at a client site in Kerala.
how_to_apply: Ensure you satisfy the eligibility conditions for the post before applying.
Apply online through the registration format available in the Career Section of the official RITES website, www.rites.com.
Note down the system-generated Registration Number displayed on the online form and use it for all further communication with RITES.
Fill in Identity Proof details carefully; the same identity document must be produced in original at later stages of selection.
Submit the application after completing the "Fill/Modify Application Form" section.
Keep a printed copy of the submitted application form and carry it at the time of selection.

Print and sign the application form containing the registration number; submit it at the time of Document Scrutiny/Interview along with self-attested photocopies of required documents in the prescribed order: photograph, updated CV/Resume, High School certificate (proof of DOB), all academic/professional certificates and mark sheets, EWS/SC/ST/OBC certificate (if applicable), identity and address proof, PAN Card, experience proof (if applicable), PWD certificate (if applicable), and any other supporting document.

No documents are to be submitted at the time of online application; candidates can apply for any number of vacancies but only one application per vacancy, and an application cannot be altered once submitted.

Hard copies of documents should not be sent by post or courier. Original testimonials along with one self-attested copy must be produced at the time of selection. SC/ST/OBC-NCL candidates must furnish caste certificates in the prescribed annexure format valid as on the cut-off date, and OBC-NCL candidates must additionally submit a non-creamy-layer declaration. EWS candidates must possess an Income and Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026-27, as per the Government of India format.
important_instructions: Management reserves the right to cancel, restrict, enlarge, modify, or alter the selection/recruitment process at any stage without prior notice. The number of vacancies may vary from what is advertised. Candidates from Government Departments/PSUs, including RITES departmental candidates, can join only after being properly relieved from their parent organisation. Furnishing false or incorrect information, or suppression of material facts, will lead to cancellation of candidature, even after appointment if detected later. Any corrigendum or addendum will be published only on www.rites.com; candidates should check the website regularly for updates. An offer letter is not proof of experience; candidates must submit a joining and relieving order/experience certificate to claim experience. Legal jurisdiction for any dispute will be Delhi. No train/bus fare, TA, or DA is payable for appearing in the selection process. Queries should be sent only to [email protected] with VC No., Registration/Roll No., candidate's full name in block letters, and a valid email address; communications missing these details will not be attended to.

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised to read the official notification carefully before applying. The direct notification PDF download link was not specified in the source document provided; candidates should check the Career section of the official RITES website at www.rites.com for the notification PDF and online application link. Download RITES Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

rows.details: RITES Limited (A Govt. of India Enterprise)
Resident Engineer (Civil), Quality Engineer (Civil), Field Engineer (Civil)

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Age, experience, and all other eligibility criteria will be reckoned as on the cut-off date, i.e. 30 July 2026, the last date for submission of the online application.

rows.particular: Commencement of submission of online application
Last date of submission of online application (VC No. CL/04/26 to CL/06/26)

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: Category-wise break-up for Field Engineer (Civil), CL/06/26: UR – 7, EWS – 1, OBC (NCL) – 2, SC – 1, ST – Not specified. Resident Engineer (CL/04/26) and Quality Engineer (CL/05/26) vacancies are entirely under the UR category, with no vacancies indicated for EWS, OBC (NCL), SC, or ST for these two posts.

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Nationality requirements were not specified in the official notification provided. Candidates are advised to verify this detail on the official RITES website before applying. The degree must be recognised by AICTE and awarded by a University established under a Central or State Act, an institution established by an Act of Parliament, or a Deemed University under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956. Sections A & B examinations of the Institution of Engineers (India), with enrolment on or before 31.05.2013, are treated as equivalent to a degree and accepted. Periods of training (other than on-the-job training), internship, teaching, academic work, fellowship, or PhD research are not counted as work experience.

rows.minimum_qualification: Full-time Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering
Full-time Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering
Full-time Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Resident Engineer (Civil) – CL/04/26: 55 years Quality Engineer (Civil) – CL/05/26: 55 years Field Engineer (Civil) – CL/06/26: 55 years Age is reckoned as on the cut-off date of 30 July 2026. PwBD candidates with not less than 40% of the relevant disability are eligible for the 10-year age relaxation, applicable irrespective of whether the post is reserved, provided the post is identified as suitable for PwBD.

rows.relaxation: Number of years of service rendered + 3 years
10 years, in addition to relaxation admissible for SC/ST/OBC-NCL where the post is identified suitable for PwBD

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: There is no application fee for any category of candidates applying for VC No. CL/04/26 to CL/06/26.

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is entirely through Interview, carrying 100% weightage — Technical & Professional proficiency: 65%, Personality, Communication & Competency: 35%. Minimum qualifying marks in the interview: 60% for UR/EWS candidates and 50% for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PWD candidates against reserved posts. There is no minimum qualifying mark required in the aggregate. Eligible candidates who successfully submit the online application are provisionally allowed for Document Scrutiny/Verification; only shortlisted/eligible candidates then appear for the Interview. RITES prepares a category-wise merit list of empanelled candidates and shares CVs with the client in order of merit. The final offer of engagement depends on approval of the CV by the client. Selected candidates must be found medically fit as per RITES Rules and Standards of Medical Fitness for the relevant post. Candidates may appear for the interview in either Hindi or English. Engagement is purely on contract basis, initially for one year, extendable until completion of the assignment subject to mutual consent and satisfactory performance. Selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India as per company requirement, though this recruitment is specifically for deployment at a client site in Kerala.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for RITES Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Ensure you satisfy the eligibility conditions for the post before applying. Apply online through the registration format available in the Career Section of the official RITES website, www.rites.com. Note down the system-generated Registration Number displayed on the online form and use it for all further communication with RITES. Fill in Identity Proof details carefully; the same identity document must be produced in original at later stages of selection. Submit the application after completing the "Fill/Modify Application Form" section. Keep a printed copy of the submitted application form and carry it at the time of selection. Print and sign the application form containing the registration number; submit it at the time of Document Scrutiny/Interview along with self-attested photocopies of required documents in the prescribed order: photograph, updated CV/Resume, High School certificate (proof of DOB), all academic/professional certificates and mark sheets, EWS/SC/ST/OBC certificate (if applicable), identity and address proof, PAN Card, experience proof (if applicable), PWD certificate (if applicable), and any other supporting document. No documents are to be submitted at the time of online application; candidates can apply for any number of vacancies but only one application per vacancy, and an application cannot be altered once submitted. Hard copies of documents should not be sent by post or courier. Original testimonials along with one self-attested copy must be produced at the time of selection. SC/ST/OBC-NCL candidates must furnish caste certificates in the prescribed annexure format valid as on the cut-off date, and OBC-NCL candidates must additionally submit a non-creamy-layer declaration. EWS candidates must possess an Income and Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026-27, as per the Government of India format.

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Remuneration
content_sections.text: Field Engineer (Civil), minimum 2 years' experience: Basic Pay ₹23,340/month; Gross Pay ₹42,478/month; Yearly CTC ₹5,09,741 Quality Engineer (Civil), minimum 5 years' experience: Basic Pay ₹25,504/month; Gross Pay ₹46,417/month; Yearly CTC ₹5,57,008 Resident Engineer (Civil), minimum 8 years' experience: Basic Pay ₹27,869/month; Gross Pay ₹50,721/month; Yearly CTC ₹6,08,658 The remuneration figures are indicative. Actual pay will depend on the place of posting and other terms and conditions of appointment.

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Management reserves the right to cancel, restrict, enlarge, modify, or alter the selection/recruitment process at any stage without prior notice. The number of vacancies may vary from what is advertised. Candidates from Government Departments/PSUs, including RITES departmental candidates, can join only after being properly relieved from their parent organisation. Furnishing false or incorrect information, or suppression of material facts, will lead to cancellation of candidature, even after appointment if detected later. Any corrigendum or addendum will be published only on www.rites.com; candidates should check the website regularly for updates. An offer letter is not proof of experience; candidates must submit a joining and relieving order/experience certificate to claim experience. Legal jurisdiction for any dispute will be Delhi. No train/bus fare, TA, or DA is payable for appearing in the selection process. Queries should be sent only to [email protected] with VC No., Registration/Roll No., candidate's full name in block letters, and a valid email address; communications missing these details will not be attended to.

faq.question: Q1. What is the RITES Recruitment 2026 notification about?
faq.answer: RITES Limited is recruiting Resident Engineer, Quality Engineer, and Field Engineer (all Civil) on contract basis through interview, for deployment at a client project site in Kerala.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 16 vacancies in total: 3 for Resident Engineer (Civil), 2 for Quality Engineer (Civil), and 11 for Field Engineer (Civil).

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for submission of the online application is 30 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. When does the online application for RITES Recruitment 2026 start?
faq.answer: Online applications commence on 10 July 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for RITES Civil Engineer posts?
faq.answer: A full-time Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering recognised by AICTE is required for all three posts.

faq.question: Q6. How much work experience is required for RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Resident Engineer requires a minimum of 8 years, Quality Engineer requires a minimum of 5 years, and Field Engineer requires a minimum of 2 years of post-qualification experience in Road/Bridges/Buildings.

faq.question: Q7. What is the age limit for RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit is 55 years for all three posts, reckoned as on the cut-off date of 30 July 2026.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any age relaxation in RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes. SC/ST candidates get 5 years, OBC-NCL candidates get 3 years, Ex-Servicemen get years of service rendered plus 3 years, J&K Domicile candidates get 5 years, and PwBD candidates get 10 years of age relaxation, subject to conditions.

faq.question: Q9. What is the application fee for RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no application fee (Nil) for any category.

faq.question: Q10. What is the selection process for RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based entirely on Interview (100% weightage), split into Technical & Professional proficiency (65%) and Personality, Communication & Competency (35%). A minimum of 60% marks is required for UR/EWS and 50% for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PWD candidates.
